items.title: Trump’s tariffs are a ‘dirty tax’ that will make the $38.6 trillion national debt crisis even worse over the long term, top analyst says
items.description: "The idea that this is pro-American is actually just the opposite," Penn Wharton's Kent Smetters told Fortune. "It hurts American manufacturers."

items.title: From mom-and-pops to Goldman Sachs, Jesse Jackson’s vision for American business sparked a revolution
items.description: The civil rights leader, whose dealmaking skills pushed Wall Street to invest in minority-owned businesses, died on Tuesday.

items.title: It’s no time to go quiet on sustainability. Just ask beauty giant L’Oreal
items.description: L’Oréal is betting millions that sustainability can drive profits as well as protect the planet.
